sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what changed after clicking on the yellow segment. The board changed with only 1 cell changed. Let me compare the before and after frames to see what happened.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after the click
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

print("BEFORE nodes:")
for node in before['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  id={node['id']:2d} color={node['color']:2s} pixels={node['pixels']:4d} rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")

print("\nAFTER nodes:")
for node in after['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  id={node['id']:2d} color={node['color']:2s} pixels={node['pixels']:4d} rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")

# Check the specific cell that changed
ascii_before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
ascii_after = last_transition.after_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for r in range(64):
    for c in range(64):
        if ascii_before[r][c] != ascii_after[r][c]:
            print(f"\nChanged at ({r},{c}): '{ascii_before[r][c]}' -> '{ascii_after[r][c]}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The click didn't change any gameplay objects - it was a wasted action. The only change was in the HUD bar.
